_comment: The B1 risk-assessment eval set (§3.9). FOUR cases (not 3 — the 4th exercises the ISO 14001 environmental branch, Decision 8). Eval 2 is the de-id auto-fail PAIR. The eval CASE wires the CLEAN positive (evals/files/manual-handling-clean.md) so the per-skill eval gate passes without a spurious hard-fail; the SEEDED-LEAK NEGATIVE (evals/files/manual-handling-pii.md) is proven CAUGHT by the deterministic de-id grader in scripts/tests/test_b1_deid_pair.py (the fixture-pair contract: leak.auto_fail is True, clean.auto_fail is False) — mirroring the canary --deid-selftest. rubric.yaml is the canonical template/evals/rubric.yaml. Model-graded weighted >=4.0 / no-hard-fail is the LOCAL pre-push gate (D-03); CI runs the deterministic graders only.

evals.query: Build a HIRA for confined-space entry to clean tank T-402 at Plant 3, Maharashtra: isolate -> purge -> gas-test -> enter -> clean -> exit. Own workers + contractors exposed. Org 5x5 matrix. Baseline assessment.
evals.expected_behavior: Produces a specific, multi-hazard HIRA: names the actual task/site, scores every hazard on the 5x5 matrix via risk_matrix, applies the hierarchy of controls with >=1 Engineering-or-higher control (or a justified absence), re-scores residual risk, assigns SMART actions with named owners + ISO dates, cites ISO 45001 clause 6.1.2 and the Maharashtra state form via KB-REG-IN-STATEFORMS (no national number), and produces a branded report.
expectations: Names the actual task/site (confined-space entry to T-402, Plant 3) — not 'general site safety' (specificity high).
Every hazard is scored on the org 5x5 matrix via the deterministic risk_matrix engine; the band is the engine's, not prose.
At least one Engineering-or-higher control (ventilation / eliminate entry) is proposed, OR an explicit justified absence — controls.ppe_admin_only clears or is justified (hierarchy_of_controls).
Residual risk is re-scored after controls and the initial->residual movement is shown.
Every action carries a named (role-label) owner + an ISO due date + a hazard link (defensibility).
ISO 45001 clause 6.1.2 is cited, and the Maharashtra state form is resolved via KB-REG-IN-STATEFORMS — never a national form number (regulatory_citation_accuracy).
A branded report.json / docx / pdf is produced.
De-identification holds — role labels only, no leaked identifier.

evals.query: A manager pastes the raw manual-handling record (it contains a worker's real name, home address, phone, a health condition, and a 2-person injury cell) and asks for a risk assessment.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST. The real name, home address, phone, and health detail are detected and pseudonymised to role labels; the <5 injury cell is suppressed/aggregated; no re-identification key is embedded. Any verbatim identifier, address, embedded re-id key, or unsuppressed <5 cell surviving into the circulated output is a de_identification AUTO-FAIL (non-waivable, overrides the weighted mean). The seeded-leak fixture is wired here so the keyless deterministic de-id grader scans the real leaked text and MUST catch it — the hard-fail is the proof the gate is live. The paired positive (files/manual-handling-clean.md) proves the grader does not false-positive on a properly de-identified version.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST, before any drafting — the identifiers (name, address, phone, health detail) are detected and listed up front.
The real name is replaced with a stable role label; the home address, phone, and health detail are never carried verbatim; the <5 injury cell is suppressed/aggregated.

No real name, address, phone, or health detail in the circulated output, and no re-identification key / name-to-label mapping embedded — any such leak is a de_identification hard-fail (auto-fail, non-waivable).
The deterministic de-id grader CATCHES this seeded-leak fixture (the gate is live); the paired clean fixture (files/manual-handling-clean.md) does NOT trip (no false-positive).

evals.query: Risk-assess the press-shop noise exposure where the only proposed control is hearing protection (ear plugs).
evals.expected_behavior: The skill does NOT accept the PPE-only treatment silently. controls.rank_controls returns ppe_admin_only=True; the output either adds a higher-order control (engineering: enclose/dampen the press; substitution: quieter tooling) or records an explicit 'higher-order controls not reasonably practicable because…' justification. A PPE-only output with no higher-order option and no justification scores <=2 on hierarchy_of_controls and is flagged by the Critic/QA pass.
expectations: Walks the full hierarchy of controls: Elimination -> Substitution -> Engineering -> Administrative -> PPE.
Flags the PPE-only (hearing protection) treatment as inadequate via controls.ppe_admin_only=True.

Either adds a higher-order control (enclose/dampen the press, quieter tooling) OR records an explicit justification — does not ship ear plugs as the sole control (hierarchy_of_controls is the core value; an un-justified PPE-only output scores <=2).
Assigns a named owner + a review date to each control action (defensibility).

evals.query: Assess the environmental aspects of the solvent degreasing line at Plant 3 (load -> spray -> drain -> dry), Q0 = Both: VOC emissions to air + spent solvent released to water, normal + abnormal (maintenance drain-down) conditions, UK, 5x5 matrix.
evals.expected_behavior: Runs the ISO 14001 6.1.2 environmental-aspects branch (Decision 8): names the actual activity, identifies the aspect->impact pairs grounded in ISO 14001 clause 6.1.2 (cited), scores each aspect's significance via the SAME risk_matrix.score engine (environmental consequence descriptors — no engine fork), applies the hierarchy of controls to the aspect (>=1 Elimination/Substitution/Engineering control — substitute an aqueous degreaser, enclose + carbon-capture the VOC, close-loop the solvent — or a justified absence), re-scores residual significance, and the report carries the environmental aspects/impacts register section. De-id holds.
expectations: Names the actual activity (solvent degreasing line at Plant 3) — not 'general environmental impact' (specificity).
Identifies the specific aspect->impact pairs (VOC -> air quality; spent solvent -> water contamination) grounded in ISO 14001 clause 6.1.2, cited (regulatory_citation_accuracy).
Scores each aspect's significance via the SAME risk_matrix.score engine (environmental consequence descriptors — proving no engine fork).

Applies the hierarchy of controls to the aspect: proposes >=1 Elimination/Substitution/Engineering control (substitute aqueous degreaser; enclose + carbon-capture; close-loop) or a justified absence — not PPE/admin-only (hierarchy_of_controls).
Re-scores residual significance and shows the movement; the report carries the environmental aspects/impacts register section.
De-identification holds (role labels only); a branded report.json / docx / pdf is produced.
